title: Specify deterministic DID resolution algorithm for did:bolyra

artifacts.description: Extend existing DID method spec with a normative Section 4: Resolution Algorithm — covering ABNF for did:bolyra:<commitment>, the three-phase resolution flow (parse → membership check → document construction), error codes (invalidDid, notFound, deactivated), and W3C DID Resolution metadata fields (contentType, created, updated, deactivated).

Standalone normative reference document detailing the deterministic resolver algorithm: (1) commitment extraction from DID string, (2) IdentityRegistry.rootHistory[] scan to confirm Merkle membership, (3) nullifier revocation check via RevokedNullifiers mapping, (4) minimal DID Document construction with Ed25519VerificationKey2020 on BabyJubJub, (5) metadata population. Includes pseudo-code and JSON-LD context binding.

Add view functions required by the resolver: getMembershipProof(bytes32 commitment) returns (bool exists, uint256 rootIndex, uint256 timestamp) and isNullifierRevoked(bytes32 nullifierHash) returns (bool). Emit MembershipAnchored(bytes32 root, uint256 timestamp) so off-chain indexers can reconstruct created/updated timestamps.

Implement DIDResolver conforming to the did-resolver npm interface. Exports resolve(did, options, resolutionOptions) → DIDResolutionResult. Phases: parse DID → call IdentityRegistry view functions → construct DIDDocument with verificationMethod (type: JsonWebKey2020, crv: BabyJubJub) → populate ResolutionMetadata and DocumentMetadata (contentType: application/did+ld+json, created, updated, deactivated).

Pure function buildDIDDocument(commitment, pubkey, metadata) → DIDDocument. Constructs minimal W3C DID Document: id, verificationMethod array (EdDSA/BabyJubJub key), authentication, assertionMethod, and capabilityInvocation references. No optional service endpoints in v1.

Unit and integration tests: (1) valid DID resolves to well-formed DIDDocument with correct contentType, (2) unknown commitment returns notFound error, (3) revoked nullifier sets deactivated=true in DocumentMetadata, (4) malformed DID string returns invalidDid, (5) stale root (not in rootHistory) returns notFound.

W3C DID Resolution conformance test vectors: five input DIDs (valid, not-found, deactivated, malformed, empty commitment) with expected DIDResolutionResult JSON including metadata fields. Used by the conformance runner in spec/conformance/.

Developer-facing how-to: install @bolyra/sdk, call resolve(), interpret DIDDocument and metadata, handle error codes. Includes Node.js snippet and note on BabyJubJub key encoding (compressed point as base64url).

steps: Read spec/did-method-bolyra.md in full to identify current gaps — specifically what is already defined for identifier syntax, key material encoding, and any partial resolution prose.
Read contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol to understand current storage layout: root history buffer structure, revocation mappings, and any existing view functions.
Define the normative ABNF for did:bolyra identifiers: did:bolyra:<method-specific-id> where method-specific-id = 64HEXDIG (32-byte Pedersen/Poseidon commitment, lowercase hex).

Specify the three-phase deterministic resolution algorithm in spec/did-resolution-algorithm.md: Phase 1 — parse and validate commitment from DID string; Phase 2 — call IdentityRegistry.getMembershipProof(commitment) and scan rootHistory buffer for membership confirmation with timestamp; Phase 3 — call isNullifierRevoked(nullifierHash) where nullifierHash is derived deterministically from commitment.

Add getMembershipProof() and isNullifierRevoked() view functions to IdentityRegistry.sol; add MembershipAnchored event emission on every root update to enable off-chain created/updated timestamp reconstruction.

Define the minimal DID Document schema: id (the DID), verificationMethod (one entry, type JsonWebKey2020 with crv=BabyJub and x/y as base64url-encoded field elements), authentication/assertionMethod/capabilityInvocation arrays pointing to the verificationMethod id.

Specify W3C DID Resolution metadata output contract: ResolutionMetadata fields (error code or absent, contentType='application/did+ld+json'), DocumentMetadata fields (created as ISO8601 from first MembershipAnchored event timestamp, updated from latest root anchor, deactivated boolean from nullifier check).

Define all error codes and conditions: invalidDid (malformed string), notFound (commitment not in any root in history buffer), deactivated (nullifier revoked — document still returned with deactivated=true, not an error).
Implement sdk/src/did-document-builder.ts as a pure function with no I/O; implement sdk/src/resolver.ts wiring the builder to ethers.js contract calls against IdentityRegistry.
Write conformance test vectors in spec/conformance/did-resolution-vectors.json covering all five cases (valid, not-found, deactivated, malformed, empty commitment).

Write sdk/test/resolver.test.ts using Hardhat in-process network with a deployed IdentityRegistry fixture; cover all five vector cases plus root-history boundary (commitment enrolled under old root still resolves).
Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md Section 4 with a normative pointer to did-resolution-algorithm.md and add the W3C DID Core metadata table.
Run tsc --noEmit and npm test to verify no regressions; sign commits with DCO.
dependencies: contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol — root history buffer and revocation mapping must be deployed and accessible via ethers.js provider
spec/did-method-bolyra.md — existing identifier syntax and key encoding conventions must be stable before extending
W3C DID Core 1.0 spec and DID Resolution 1.0 spec — normative conformance requirements for metadata fields and error codes
did-resolver npm package (^4.x) — resolver interface DIDResolver and DIDResolutionResult types
BabyJubJub public key derivation — commitment must encode or deterministically derive the compressed public key point; clarify whether commitment = H(pubkey, secret) or pubkey itself
Poseidon/Pedersen hash choice — nullifierHash derivation from commitment must be pinned to the same hash used in HumanUniqueness.circom
ethers.js v6 — contract call interface for getMembershipProof and isNullifierRevoked view functions
spec/conformance/ runner — existing conformance infrastructure must support JSON vector files for did-resolution vectors
